steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The subject is "Coaches in the IWBF." "Coaches" is plural; the prepositional phrase "in the IWBF" just modifies it. The blank needs a verb that agrees with a plural subject in simple present tense (the sentence describes an ongoing practice, not a single past event).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — is using: singular present progressive. "Is" agrees with a singular subject; "Coaches" is plural. ✗
  • Choice B — has used: singular present perfect. "Has" agrees with a singular subject; fails plural agreement. ✗
  • Choice C — use: bare plural present. Correctly agrees with the plural subject "Coaches" and fits the simple present context. ✓
  • Choice D — uses: third-person singular present. Requires a singular subject; "Coaches" is plural. ✗
That matches Choice C — use.
